channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Bill O'Reilly: Trump will use drones if cartels don't withdraw
item.description: "If they don't recede, you're gonna see dead bodies all over the place and that's coming soon," O'Reilly says.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: Rubio says El Salvador offers to accept deportees from US of any nationality, including Americans
item.description: SAN SALVADOR, El Salvador (AP) — U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio said late Monday that El Salvador's president has offered to accept deportees from the U.S. of any nationality, including violent American criminals now imprisoned in the United States.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and JUAN ZAMORANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Elon Musk creates confusion about IRS' Direct File
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Billionaire tech mogul Elon Musk posted Monday on his social media site that he had “deleted” 18F, a government agency that worked on technology projects such as the IRS’ Direct File program.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How can Trump achieve campaign pledge to eliminate Dept of Education? Experts weigh in
item.description: Fox News Digital spoke to experts this week about Trump's plan to eliminate the Department of Education, what that process looks like, and what obstacles Trump faces.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: California Assembly approves $50M to defend immigrants and protect state policies from Trump plans
item.description: California’s Democratic-dominated Assembly has endorsed up to $50 million in funding to defend the state’s progressive policies against challenges by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Sophie Austin, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Senate tees up final vote on Pam Bondi AG nomination
item.description: The Senate on Monday teed up a final vote on Pam Bondi's nomination to lead the Justice Department. The chamber voted 52-46 to limit debate. Absent an agreement with Democrats, that puts a final confirmation vote on the docket for the early hours of Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Regina Zilbermints, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump agriculture nominee, Brooke Rollins, clears key Senate hurdle ahead of confirmation vote
item.description: Brooke Rollins, the former president and CEO of the America First Policy Institute, had earned broad support from farmers and growers' groups ahead of her Senate confirmation vote.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration hires hardcore pro-Trump ideologue to run public diplomacy at State Department
item.description: SAN SALVADOR, El Salvador (AP) — The Trump administration has hired a “Make America Great Again” ideologue to run the State Department’s worldwide public diplomacy efforts, according to the man put forward for the post and three current department officials.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: GOP's Susan Collins to back Tulsi Gabbard confirmation
item.description: Sen. Susan Collins (R-Maine) announced Monday that she will support Tulsi Gabbard to become director of national intelligence (DNI), giving her a key boost in her confirmation push.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Elon Musk tightens grip on federal government as Democrats raise alarms
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Elon Musk is rapidly consolidating control over large swaths of the federal government with President Donald Trump ’s blessing, sidelining career officials, gaining access to sensitive databases and dismantling a leading source of humanitarian assistance.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chris Wright confirmed to serve as Trump's Secretary of Energy
item.description: Chris Wright, CEO of Liberty Energy, was confirmed by the Senate to serve as secretary of Energy under President Donald Trump's administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NYC mayor downplays economic concerns of Trump deportation plan
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- There have anecdotal reports that immigration raids are driving undocumented New Yorkers into the shadows. New York City has more than 400,000 undocumented workers who help prop up the economy; however, Mayor Eric Adams said he was not worried they would stop working.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Trump administration opens antisemitism inquiries at 5 colleges including Columbia and Berkeley
item.description: The Trump administration is opening new investigations into allegations of antisemitism at five U.S. universities including Columbia and the University of California, Berkeley.
item.dc:creator: Collin Binkley, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump pauses Canadian tariffs for 30 days in deal with Trudeau
item.description: President Trump and Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau agreed to a pause in implementing tariffs for at least a month, just hours after Trump agreed to delay tariffs on Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New York AG Letitia James advises hospitals to ignore Trump EO about sex-change procedures for minors
item.description: New York Attorney General Letitia James warned hospitals to continue providing sex-change procedures for minors, despite President Trump's EO prohibiting it.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Thune on Trump tariffs: 'We’ll see how long' they last
item.description: Senate Majority Leader John Thune (R-S.D.) told reporters Monday he’s taking a wait-and-see approach to President Trump’s order to place 25-percent tariffs on Canada and Mexico and a 10-percent tariff on China, which sent stock markets dropping.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump doubles down on floating Canada as 51st state amid tariff dispute
item.description: President Trump on Monday reiterated his desire to see Canada become part of the United States as he prepared to impose steep tariffs on Canadian goods.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump agrees to pause tariffs on Canada and Mexico after they pledge to boost border enforcement
item.description: President Donald Trump is holding off on his tariff threats against Mexico and Canada for 30 days after the two U.S. neighbors agreed to boost border security efforts.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: As Ken Martin takes helm of Democratic National Committee, DFL faces opening at the top
item.description: Democrats from around the country picked Martin to lead them forward over the weekend. Minnesota Democrats will have a similar discussion next month.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Elon Musk says President Donald Trump has 'agreed' USAID should be shut down
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. Agency for International Development is on the cusp of being shuttered, according the Trump administration's billionaire adviser and Tesla CEO Elon Musk — who has been wrestling for control of the agency in recent days.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump imposes tariffs on Canada, Mexico and China, raising prospect of higher costs for U.S. consumers
item.description: President Donald Trump on Saturday signed an order to impose stiff tariffs on imports from Mexico, Canada and China — fulfilling one of his post-campaign commitments to voters that also carries the risk of sparking higher inflation and disrupting businesses across North America.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: How non-violent undocumented immigrants could be swept up in raids
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – In two weeks of the second Trump administration, the White House is staying true to tough immigration policies with ICE raids across the country. Several have happened in the tri-state area.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Trump’s FCC chief opens investigation into NPR and PBS
item.description: Brendan Carr, the new head of the Federal Communications Commission under President Trump, says the public broadcasters are being investigated for allegedly running commercials.
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik, MPR News

item.title: Weekend vote to decide if Ken Martin jumps from Minnesota Democratic perch to national party head
item.description: Members of the Democratic National Committee are due to vote Saturday on who will lead the party. After the 2024 election, Democrats are regrouping.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Lawmakers clash over immigration policies, in-state tuition for Dreamers
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (WFLA) — As Gov. Ron DeSantis and legislative leaders in Tallahassee clash on immigration proposals, DeSantis said he's planning to veto the recently passed TRUMP Act. That move could put lawmakers back to square one.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Who is Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy? You may recognize him from MTV
item.description: Duffy, formerly a reality TV star and Wisconsin congressman, was confirmed as the new transportation secretary on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Jill Colvin, Fox 5

item.title: Trump blames diversity hiring as probe into deadly D.C. plane crash begins
item.description: As the nation reeled from the deadliest American aviation disaster in more than two decades, President Donald Trump on Thursday baselessly blamed diversity initiatives for undermining air safety and questioned the actions of a U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: White House budget office rescinds federal funding freeze memo
item.description: The Trump administration’s Office of Management and Budget released a new memo Wednesday rescinding a controversial memo issued late Monday that froze a wide swath of federal financial assistance, which had paralyzed many federal programs and caused a huge uproar on Capitol Hill.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Trump administration to offer buyouts to all federal workers ahead of return to office
item.description: The Trump administration is offering all 2 million federal employees what amounts to a buyout if they do not intend to return to work in person later this year, sources confirmed to The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WFLA, NBC

item.title: States lose access to Medicaid payment portal amid Trump federal aid freeze
item.description: State Medicaid programs across the country are reporting they've lost access to federal payment portals one day after President Trump announced a freeze on federal grants and aid.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, Fox 5

item.title: Ousted IG: Trump firings a 'a potentially existential threat' to government oversight
item.description: One of 17 government watchdogs ousted by President Trump said that the move was a “a potentially existential threat” to a crucial government oversight tool.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Federal judge temporarily blocks Trump admin's freeze on federal grants, loans
item.description: A federal judge has temporarily blocked a Trump administration freeze on federal grants and loans that could total trillions of dollars.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump's Nevada voters united on top issues while Harris backers couldn't, NVPOP poll shows
item.description: A new poll that examines the driving forces behind Nevada voters' decisions in the 2024 election shows that President Donald Trump's support came from people who were focused on two primary issues: the economy and immigration.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump signs order that likely sets in motion a future ban on transgender troops
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump signed an executive order Monday directing Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth to revise the Pentagon’s policy on transgender troops, likely setting in motion a future ban on their military service.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP and LOLITA C. BALDOR Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
